structural_claim: Topological constraints fundamentally determine the evolution and stability of dynamical systems, with topology being conserved except at singular surfaces where it changes through specific mechanisms

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Spacetime topology must contain singularities under generic conditions, representing unavoidable topological features
Gravitational collapse erases all information except topological charges (mass, charge, angular momentum)
Magnetic field topology is conserved in ideal plasmas, with field lines frozen into the fluid
Topological linkage of magnetic field lines is conserved in ideal MHD, constraining plasma evolution
Magnetic topology changes only at singular current sheets where ideal constraints break down
